Figure 9: Given a target attribute distribution as meta5-Uniform, this figure provides a qualitative comparison of samples generated by different methods in the CIFAR experiment reported in Table 1. The top block shows the target attribute distribution. Each block along the vertical direction exhibits samples generated by one method, clustered by the samples’ attribute values, and the empirical attribute distribution is also marked.
Figure 1: An overview of our proposed method for aligning attribute distribution.
Table 1: Quantitative evaluation metrics (all lower the better) on CIFAR-100. Best in bold, secondbest underlined.
Figure 2: Top row: Test-time target attribute distributions (CIFAR-100). 2nd/3rd rows: Generated distributions of baselines. Bottom row: Generated distributions of our method.

Table 7: Hyperparameters used for our method (flow-based) in human face generation.

Figure 7: Total Variation (top left), FID (top right), Jensen-Shannon divergence, and χ2 divergence metrics with different batch size M when target is ZigZag.

Figure 3: Qualitative samples from a single batch of our method (right), compared to vanilla DDIM (left) and PG (mid) for generating human faces with a fair target distribution over age groups (Uniform-age), where the ratio of faces in three age groups should be 1:1:1. The pretrained diffusion model learned a highly imbalanced age distribution from the FFHQ dataset, where most faces are classified as Middle. Our approach aligns the generated attribute distribution with the target by minimally editing facial details such as wrinkles and face shapes. See more samples in Appendix D.2.
Figure 11: Additional samples from all methods in the face generation experiment.
Figure 6: Total Variation (top left), FID (top right), Jensen-Shannon divergence, and χ2 divergence metrics with different batch size M when target is Gaussian.
Table 3: Quantitative results for face generation with joint attribute control. Best in bold, second-best underlined.
Table 2: Quantitative evaluation metrics on face generation with controlling single attribute. Best in bold, second-best underlined. OURS-F denotes latent-flow-based version of our method.
